Defendants Maria Rios, Alexis Cortes, Victor Rios And Angel Cortes' Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-20-585658 SHIRLEY YANHUA YU VS. FRANKLIN M. QUESADA ET AL and
CGC-20-585659 SHIRLEY YANHUA YU VS. MARIA ISABEL RIOS ET AL

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Justin Goodman (justin@zfplaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Steve Collier (415-771-9850 x 1122; steve@thclinic.org) of Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc. for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Maria Rios, Alexis Cortes, Victor Rios And Angel Cortes' Motion To Strike Complaint is denied. Defendants do not demonstrate that paragraph nineteen of the Complaint and paragraph three of Plaintiff's Prayer for Relief are "not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 436(b).)

The Court declines to find that any contract between the parties is illegal as Plaintiff's Notice of Intent to Withdraw states only that Plaintiff "believes" the premises is unauthorized. Defendants do not submit any evidence establishing that Defendants' unit is an unauthorized unit as defined by San Francisco Planning Code sec. 317(b)(13).

Defendants fail to cite authority that prohibits Plaintiff from alleging holdover damages when a Shelter-in-Place Order is in effect, nor do they show that it was impossible for them to move after their tenancy expired on April 24, 2020. The issue of damages is one for the trier of fact.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
